2605.18007.title: Semantic Reranking at Inference Time for Hard Examples in Rhetorical Role Labeling
2605.18007.paper_summary: Rhetorical Role Labeling (RRL) assigns a functional role to each sentence in a document and is widely used in legal, medical, and scientific domains. While language models (LMs) achieve strong average performance, they remain unreliable on hard examples, where prediction confidence is low. Existing approaches typically handle uncertainty implicitly and treat labels as discrete identifiers, overlooking the semantic information encoded in label names. We introduce RISE, an inference-time semantic reranking framework that leverages label semantics to refine predictions on hard instances. RISE automatically identifies low-confidence predictions and reranks model outputs using contrastively learned label representations, without retraining or modifying the underlying model. Experiments on eight domain-specific RRL datasets with seven LMs, including encoder-based and causal architectures, show an average gain of +9.15 macro-F1 points on hard examples. For explainability, we further propose manual hardness annotations to study difficulty from both model and human perspectives, revealing a moderate agreement with Cohen's kappa = 0.40.
2605.18007.paper_summary_zh: 修辭角色標記 (RRL) 為文件中的每個句子分配一個功能角色，並廣泛應用於法律、醫療和科學領域。雖然語言模型 (LMs) 在平均性能上表現良好，但在困難範例上仍然不可靠，預測信心較低。現有的方法通常隱式處理不確定性，並將標籤視為離散識別符，忽略了標籤名稱中編碼的語義信息。我們引入了 RISE，一種推理時語義重排序框架，利用標籤語義來精煉困難實例的預測。RISE 自動識別低信心預測，並使用對比學習的標籤表示對模型輸出進行重排序，而無需重新訓練或修改基礎模型。在八個特定領域的 RRL 數據集上進行的實驗中，使用七個 LMs，包括基於編碼器和因果架構，顯示在困難範例上平均增益為 +9.15 macro-F1 點。為了增強可解釋性，我們進一步提出手動困難度標註，從模型和人類的角度研究難度，顯示出中等的一致性，Cohen's kappa = 0.40。
2605.18007.author: Anas Belfathi et.al.
2605.18007.authors: Anas Belfathi, Nicolas Hernandez, Laura Monceaux, Warren Bonnard, Richard Dufour
